title: The Great Peg Escape
tagline: Stuck in the carpet fibers? Not for long.
synopsis: After being accidentally dropped behind the radiator, a lone yellow Farmer must navigate a dusty, mountainous landscape of discarded pet hair and lost buttons to return to his primary-colored school bus. His only obstacle is a sheer four-inch climb up a shag rug that lacks any built-in peg-holes for his base.
fun_fact: The lead stunt-peg had to be glued to a motorized base because he kept falling over whenever the air conditioning vent blew on the set.
poster_prompt: A yellow peg-person staring up at a towering wall of giant gray carpet fibers, a discarded metal button laying in the dust nearby, bright studio light casting a long dramatic shadow, vintage 1970s toy style, classic cylindrical peg people with two dots for eyes and a small curved painted-on smile, simple solid primary color bodies, smooth glossy plastic and wooden textures, studio lighting, macro product toy photography, beautiful creative composition, tilt-shift lens look

title: Love at the Plug-Hole
tagline: A connection that just doesn't fit.
synopsis: A blue-hatted Nurse and a red-hatted Doctor share a longing glance across the kitchen floor, but their rigid cylindrical shapes make physical intimacy impossible without someone physically picking them up to touch heads. Will they ever find a custom-molded base that allows them to hold hands?
fun_fact: The love scene took three days to film because the props department kept accidentally swapping the red hat for a green one, causing severe continuity errors.
poster_prompt: Two peg-people facing each other on a shiny white and red checkerboard floor, one with a blue hat and one with a red hat, a plastic toy heart accessory placed between them, vintage 1970s toy style, classic cylindrical peg people with two dots for eyes and a small curved painted-on smile, simple solid primary color bodies, smooth glossy plastic and wooden textures, studio lighting, macro product toy photography, beautiful creative composition, tilt-shift lens look

title: The Vacuum Menace
tagline: The roar of the motor is the sound of your doom.
synopsis: An entire neighborhood of plastic peg-people watches in terror as a thundering mechanical beast with a rotating brush-roll begins swallowing everything in its path. With no limbs to hold onto the furniture, the residents must find a way to wedge themselves into the safety of a non-removable baseplate before they are sucked into the dust bin.
fun_fact: The 'vacuum' sound effect was actually just a crew member humming loudly into a plastic cup while shaking a box of dry cereal.

title: Pegs in Space
tagline: To reach the top of the shelf, you must go beyond.
synopsis: In a future where the living room is a vast galaxy, a brave Astronaut pilot discovers that his vehicle's plastic steering wheel is purely decorative and he cannot actually turn the craft. He must rely on the giant hand of fate—a toddler—to steer his ship toward the final frontier: the toy box.
fun_fact: The 'stars' in the background were actually just glitter glued to a piece of black construction paper that kept falling down every five minutes.

title: The Long Range Walk
tagline: It's just five inches, but it feels like a marathon.
synopsis: A weary Sheriff with a painted-on mustache rides his sturdy wooden horse across the scorching plains of the hardwood floor. He faces off against a band of outlaws who have maliciously tipped over every plastic fence in town, forcing him to stand perfectly still for hours in the midday sun.
fun_fact: The horse was cast from a vintage mold, but the Sheriff kept falling off the saddle because his peg-hole was slightly too shallow for the horse's mounting post.
